author: @rogerbraintree9552
text:
I have absolutely no idea how you are able to work so quickly.  It probably helps having a workshop whereas I have to do everything in my flat so any project I do has to  be very small.  But looking at the Hofler engine reminds me of another type of resonance engine which uses solenoid actuated valves.  If you stand behind a car exhaust pipe, as the escape valves open and close releasing exhaust gasses, the exhaust system acts like a huge resonator and so you can feel the thrust of air beating out of the exhaust pipe.  Of course, the exhaust system has been designed to cancel and absorb resonance so it's an antiresonator.  but, by boiling a small amount of water under pressure and releasing the pressure build up through an actuated valve would produce quite a popping sound!

However, the difficult bit is making the valve because unlike a washing machine actuated water inlet valve, it needs to operate at significantly above 100 degrees C.  Finding a bit of junk that has the valve in question is somewhat tricky, especially as it probably wont be actuated.  There may be some car valves for the cooling system, but they tend to temperature dependant, so I'm stuck with having to bore a hole in a piece of brass and fit a shaft that is precise enough to provide a good air seal.  I'll probably have to make a special drill bit to do the job so this will take me a good long time and a lot of effort.  I'll have to build 2 of these, one to blow air into a chamber to push against the linear generator and another to empty the chamber.  These would be electronically controlled and adjusted until the opening and closing of the valves resonate with the chamber.  Anyway it's going to take me forever to design and build this valve so that it can have pipes screwed into it.  I just don't know how you manage to knock things up so fast.

replies.author: @al3xf103
replies.text: Best of luck with this project ! Maybe breaking every single step down at the planning stage and then ticking each one off as it gets done might help, as it’s helped me. Then it doesn’t matter how fast you work as long as you make progress. If you make a video about it, do let us know in these comments! Cheers

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: It is all I do and as I only sleep 4 hours a night I work twice as long as a 'normal' working day - I also build the projects with the things I find rather than having to look for specific things - that makes it much quicker - what about push rod ball valves seated against a HT O ring?

author: @jasonhaymanonthedrawingboard
text: Love it! You actually built it! I hope you realise that can be Used for wind energy as well? if it’s beefed up alittle more! I’ve seen design with inward facing blades. As big as a hamster wheel.  Heck, a mag lev hamster wheel turbine perhaps? Renewables as well marvellous!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I didn't realise that - until now! thanks for the mind worm mate lol

replies.author: @jasonhaymanonthedrawingboard
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering fantastic! Glad you liked it! It is called a tam turbine.  If I recall right they had one that was 3m in diameter.  Which is spot on for what you have built. Definitely worth a look and play with. You have the added advantage of being able to rig external fins to the gully of the tread wheel. To make it a both run on wind and water.
